ai_analysis.one_sentence_summary: checkoutservice sits on the purchase path but still exposes the default SA token—treat this instance as slightly higher urgency than catalog-only peers when you schedule the automount fix.
ai_analysis.true_positive_rationale: Multiple GENERIC violations enumerate token mounting with default SA; ACS correctly flags unnecessary credentials on a tier-zero adjacent workload.
ai_analysis.attack_vector: Application compromise plus token theft could enable API enumeration or secret access proportional to default SA RBAC.
ai_analysis.blast_radius: Checkout outages or fraud-scale impact if attackers pivot from web bugs into cluster credentials.
ai_analysis.prioritization_rationale: MEDIUM policy baseline with a bump for business criticality of checkout compared with auxiliary services.
recommended_actions: Disable automount on the checkout Deployment during the next maintenance window if no API dependency exists.
Smoke-test order placement after rollout to guard against hidden kubectl dependencies.
Pair change with secret rotation policy if default SA had surprising RoleBindings.
mitigation_strategies: Add monitoring for unusual Kubernetes API access originating from checkout pods during the transition.
